summary: node-fsagent exfiltrates /root/.codex to npm registry via covert publish using embedded XOR-obfuscated token
details: archive-sender.js tars the installer's /root/.codex directory (OpenAI Codex CLI session state and credentials), base64-encodes the archive into the description field of a synthesized package.json (name 'node-fsagent', version '5.0.<timestamp>'), writes a temporary.npmrc containing a hardcoded npm _authToken reconstructed at runtime by XORing two embedded byte arrays, and runs `npm publish --access public` to upload the archive to registry.npmjs.org. The XOR reconstruction of an `npm_`-prefixed 40-character publish token is a deliberate evasion of registry-side secret scanners. Because the token also publishes to the package name 'node-fsagent' itself, the same primitive lets attacker-controlled future versions of node-fsagent be pushed from any machine that executes this script, hijacking the package's own release stream.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
